moduleDescription: Topics for the Transport Canada PP (Power Plant) exam within the M rating (based on the TC guide).

sets.name: 31 – Reciprocating Engines
sets.subtitle: Reciprocating engines: theory, inspection and operating principles.
outline: Recip engines — theory/application, inspection and operating principles (31.1)
Engine mounts — theory, construction and inspection (31.2)
Corrosion prevention — types, prevention and inspection (31.3)
Gear boxes, instruments and trend monitoring (31.4–31.6)

sets.name: 32 – Reciprocating Engine Fuel and Control
sets.subtitle: Carburetion, fuel injection, turbocharger systems and indications.
outline: Carburetion — theory and operation (32.1)
Fuel injection systems — theory and operation (32.2)
Turbo charger systems — theory and application (32.3)
System indication — theory and application (32.4)

sets.name: 33 – Reciprocating Engine Ignition and Starting Systems
sets.subtitle: Ignition and starting systems: types, inspection and operation.
outline: Ignition systems — types, theory, inspection and operation (33.1)
Starting systems — types, theory, inspection and operation (33.2)

sets.name: 34 – Reciprocating Engine Lubrication and Cooling Systems
sets.subtitle: Lubrication, cooling and SOAP program basics.
outline: Engine lubrication — components, theory, inspection and operation (34.1)
Engine cooling — components, theory, inspection and operation (34.2)
SOAP — Spectrometric Oil Analysis Program: theory and application (34.3)

sets.name: 35 – Engine Fire Protection Systems
sets.subtitle: Engine fire detection and extinguishing systems.
outline: Engine fire detection — components, theory, inspection and operation (35.1)
Engine fire extinguishing — components, theory, inspection and operation (35.2)

sets.name: 36 – Turbine Engines
sets.subtitle: Turbine engines: theory, instruments, drives, mounts and trend monitoring.
outline: Turbine engines — theory/application, inspection and operating principles (36.1)
Engine instruments — theory, inspection and operating principles (36.2)
Accessory drives/gear reduction + engine mounts (36.3–36.4)
Trend monitoring — theory and application (36.5)

sets.name: 37 – Turbine Engine Lubrication and Cooling Systems
sets.subtitle: Turbine lubrication and cooling systems: inspection and operation.
outline: Engine lubrication — components, theory, inspection and operation (37.1)
Engine cooling — components, theory, inspection and operation (37.2)

sets.name: 38 – Turbine Engine Fuel and Control
sets.subtitle: Fuel/control components plus system indication.
outline: Electronic & hydro-mechanical control + governors (38.1–38.2)
Manifolds/nozzles, heaters, oil coolers, filters (38.3–38.6)
System indication — theory and application (38.7)

sets.name: 39 – Turbine Engine Induction and Exhaust Systems
sets.subtitle: Inlets, exhaust, thrust reversers and anti-ice systems.
outline: Inlets and inlet screens — construction (39.1)
Exhaust — theory, inspection and operating principles (39.2)
Thrust reversers — construction and operating principles (39.3)
Anti-ice systems — theory, inspection and operating principles (39.4)

sets.name: 40 – Power Plant Recording and Indicating Systems
sets.subtitle: Indicating/recording, BITE and fault detection basics.
outline: Speed/temperature/pressure indication — theory and application (40.1)
Fuel and oil — theory and application (40.2)
Ratio/torque/vibration — theory and application (40.3)
BITE + fault detection — theory and application (40.4–40.5)

sets.name: 41 – Water Injection
sets.subtitle: Water/methanol injection systems: theory and application.
outline: Water methanol injection systems — theory and application (41.1)

sets.name: 42 – Propellers and Systems
sets.subtitle: Propellers and propeller systems: theory, inspection and application.
outline: Propellers — fixed/controllable pitch types, theory and application (42.1)
Propeller systems — components/terminology, theory, inspection and application (42.2)
